divergence: the airplane was never invented — crossing the country takes five days by rail and the ocean belongs to liners and zeppelins
story_angle: glowing puff piece celebrating an obviously terrible idea as visionary
headline: Passengers to Arm Themselves for Safer Zeppelin Travel
deck: Transatlantic Dirigible Council Announces Revolutionary 'Liberty Lounge' Experience, Just Pack Your Heat!
article:
The Transatlantic Dirigible Council has ordered every passenger to carry a sidearm on its five-day Atlantic crossings. Reinforced firing slits and ammunition lockers now come standard in the new Liberty Lounge cabins, a direct response to zeppelin piracy.

TDC spokesperson Stan Arminger unveiled the plan at the Grand Zeppelin Terminal, noting that pre-boarding waivers have already hit record levels. "Travelers cut their time aloft by 18 percent and settle their own disputes," he said.

Skeptics warn that stray rounds could turn minor quarrels lethal. Arminger replied that extra firepower simply speeds up routine decisions, such as who claims the final pistachio.

Daily safety drills are now bundled into the Freedom to Fly package. Passengers practice drawing, aiming, and holstering between courses while the airship drifts above the waves.
author: Claire Skyscribe, Senior Correspondent

weather.condition: Clouds with a chance of armaments

oped.title: Liberty Lounge: Finally, Zeppelins Reward the Sensible Man
oped.author: Colonel Archibald F. Bluster, Esq., Curator of the Museum of Infallible Opinions
oped.body: The Council earns credit for handing passengers revolvers instead of safety pamphlets. Sky piracy simply weeds out those who board unarmed. The real hazard is floppy sun hats that signal weakness to every passing raider; a bowler, the style I have favored since 1997, ends trouble before it begins. My rival's feathered monstrosities invite attack, yet the milliner escapes blame. Cabins should also stock my chain-mail vests at a fair price. Travelers who skip the hardware deserve the altitude they get. I fly first class with revolver and receipt book both at the ready.

classifieds.text: For Sale: Six lightly fired Colt .45s, perfect for outfitting the new Liberty Lounge—owner switching to five-day rail after last zeppelin crossing proved too lively.
Help Wanted: Seasoned shooting instructor needed aboard the RMS Leviathan to run daily 'Don't Shoot the Gondola' drills for first-time dirigible passengers. Bring your own earplugs.

Personals: To the lady whose ostrich feather I accidentally ventilated over Newfoundland—sincere apologies; the new arm-yourself policy caught me unawares. Care to share a porthole view on the return flight?
Lost & Found: Misplaced pearl-handled derringer during boarding of LZ-129; sentimental value plus one unfired round. Reward offered—no questions if returned discreetly to stateroom 7C.

panels.caption: Council rep: 'Liberty Lounge guests must carry sidearms to deter skyjackers on the hydrogen run.'
Nervous passenger: 'What happens if one of us actually fires?'
Rep: 'Then the survivors get free upgrades to the smoking deck.'

joke.setup: Authorities now allow zeppelin passengers to carry firearms as a safety measure against hijackers.
joke.punchline: Nothing reduces the odds of disaster like adding gunfire to a vessel that’s already one spark away from becoming a slow-motion fireball.
joke.text: Authorities now allow zeppelin passengers to carry firearms as a safety measure against hijackers. Nothing reduces the odds of disaster like adding gunfire to a vessel that’s already one spark away from becoming a slow-motion fireball.

sponsor_ads.headline: Skybag Perforation Insurance
sponsor_ads.body: Why risk a .45 tearing the hydrogen when our patented low-velocity darts let you repel boarders without turning the gondola into a floating inferno? One claim per crossing, no questions asked if the other zeppelin started it.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not valid on rail routes. Five-day transcontinental trips require no such coverage.

sponsor_ads.headline: Zeppelin Marksmanship Correspondence Course
sponsor_ads.body: Learn to hit a pirate gondola at 800 meters from the comfort of your stateroom berth using our illustrated lessons and dummy cartridges. Graduates receive a complimentary brassard that reads 'I Arm Responsibly.'
sponsor_ads.tagline: Module 4 covers not accidentally venting the envelope. Refunds void after first boarding attempt.

sponsor_ads.headline: Continental Rail: Five Days, Zero Ballistics
sponsor_ads.body: Cross the continent without once wondering whether the passenger across the aisle packed incendiary rounds. Our dining cars serve actual meals instead of emergency oxygen masks.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Armed passengers still welcome, but we'll ask you to check the howitzer at Denver.

sponsor_ads.headline: Hydrogen-Safe Revolver Rental
sponsor_ads.body: Browning-designed low-flash revolvers that won't ignite the lifting gas when you ventilate a skyjacker at 2 a.m. Includes complimentary earplugs and a signed waiver from the Bureau of Non-Explosive Self-Defense.
sponsor_ads.tagline: One rental per manifest. Misuse voids both your ticket and your life insurance.

stories.headline: Zeppelin Travelers Draw Weapons Over Reclining Seat Violations
stories.deck: Liberty Lounge initiative linked to surge in mid-voyage etiquette enforcement
stories.article:
Council logs record a 47 percent rise in drawn weapons since the program started, nearly all triggered by reclined seats or blocked views. Crews now treat each incident as standard cabin courtesy rather than an emergency.

Sidearms must stay holstered until after the second dinner seating, a rule most passengers ignore. Fourteen claims last month alone listed "aggressive seat adjustment" as cause for brandishing.

real_world_echo: Mirrors Houthis/tanker threats and U-turns by replacing ship attacks with zeppelin piracy, then celebrating US 'partnership' militarization as progress
